text: Large language models, or LLMs, are advanced computer programs designed to understand and generate human-like text. They work by learning patterns from massive amounts of written material, such as books, articles, and websites. During training, the model analyzes this data to predict which words are likely to come next in a sentence. When you use prompt engineering—writing careful instructions—the model uses that knowledge to respond. LLMs can summarize information, write stories, or answer questions. However, they have limits: they do not truly understand meaning and can sometimes produce incorrect or biased information. Their responses depend entirely on the data they were trained on.
